channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Jagex Marks RuneScape: Dragonwilds' First Anniversary and Announces Service Expansion to Asia-Pacific Region
item.content:encoded:
RuneScape: Dragonwilds is marking its first anniversary in Early Access. Jagex continues its big plans for the RuneScape IP, saying that it will bring the successful survival crafting MMO to the Asia-Pacific service region later this year.

In a first anniversary video, the team goes back even further, to when they decided to expand upon the RuneScape IP and developed the concept that became Dragonwilds. This began about two years ago, with those desires to expand the franchise and, in the process, hopefully attract some new players to Gielinor. 

They decided to “create a survival game that brought RuneScape to life in a modern engine, vividly and thoroughly. A true Iron Man experience…That was something we'd heard from players for many years. We'd always heard that the survival and life sim were the things you'd want from a new RuneScape game”. 

After tens of thousands of alpha testers, Early Access began a year ago, and the game sold more than a million copies. Jagex revealed that number as part of their RS25 announcements in January, and so it is more than that now. 

As the video goes into, they've continued working on the world, improvements, and they've added lots of quests, two new regions, Fellhollow and, just last month, Dowdun Reach. The team also added their first, Farming, with the second, Fishing,  on the way. They are working to deliver what they have announced on their roadmap, including the next two regions on the way, Umbrella Sands and the Scorned Wilderness.  We can also continue expecting new quests, additional monthly content, and more.

After expanding to Latin America last fall, this year, they will add Asia-Pacific as the next service region. This means that the game will be localized into Simplified Chinese, Japanese, and Korean. There's no exact window on when this will happen other than later this year, but RS25 is already ambitious so we expect to hear more about this before too long. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/jagex-marks-runescape-dragonwilds-first-anniversary-and-announces-service-expansion-to-asia-pacific-region-2000137817
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
